commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=af9a45358dad3685b8b77178e89dad382235d18d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the WinUI project fallback `<Version>` from 0.4.7 to 0.5.0 and replaces one A2UI dashboard test version literal with `AppVersionInfo.DisplayVersion`.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main has `<Version>0.4.7</Version>` and the A2UI dashboard fixture still hardcodes `v0.4.7`. I did not run a WinUI build because this review is read-only and build/test commands would write artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer policy judgment plus real behavior proof, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: [Repo Assist] fix(version): bump csproj Version to 0.5.0 and fix hardcoded version in UI test
target.body:
🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Fixes the stale version number reported in the UI for local (non-CI) builds.

## Root cause

`<Version>0.4.7</Version>` in `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.csproj` was never updated when the v0.5.0 release tag was created. In CI builds GitVersion overrides this with `-p:Version=...`, so CI artefacts are correct — but local builds and any path that falls through to the embedded assembly attribute report `v0.4.7`.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.csproj` | `<Version>0.4.7</Version>` → `0.5.0` |
| `tests/OpenClaw.Tray.UITests/A2UIDashboardScaleTest.cs` | Replace `Lit("v0.4.7")` with `Lit(AppVersionInfo.DisplayVersion)` so the assertion stays correct across future bumps |

## Rationale

- v0.5.0 is the latest stable release tag (`git tag` shows `v0.5.0` and `v0.6.0-alpha.1`). Bumping to 0.5.0 aligns the local-build fallback with the current stable release.
- Using `AppVersionInfo.DisplayVersion` in the UI test eliminates the need to touch this file on every version bump.

## Trade-offs

- A `v0.6.0-alpha.1` tag also exists; if the project has already moved to targeting 0.6.0 as the working version, the csproj could be bumped to `0.6.0-alpha.1` instead. This PR takes the conservative approach of matching the latest stable release.

## Test Status

- ✅ `dotnet build` on `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` and `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` — succeeded
- ✅ `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` — 858 passed, 2 skipped (infrastructure-only skips)
- ⚠️ `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` — 8 pre-existing failures (Windows path tests that fail on Linux runner, e.g. `C:\\` vs `/`); same count before and after this change

Closes #570
